context.description: Reflective prose on forgetting, memory, and widened awareness. It contrasts fleeting self-observation with durable impressions and cites Ouspensky and Gurdjieff in the section "Perlen in der Dunkelheit".
normalization_notes: Chapter-like prose page with section heading 'Perlen in der Dunkelheit'.

rows.paragraph_text: Momentary success is quickly forgotten, leading to renewed frustration and a sense that the effort was wasted.
rows.text_content: Momentary success is quickly forgotten, leading to renewed frustration and a sense that the effort was wasted.

rows.paragraph_text: A later intention is also forgotten almost immediately, along with what was meant to be done.
rows.text_content: A later intention is also forgotten almost immediately, along with what was meant to be done.

rows.paragraph_text: Memory is described as sparse and episodic, with rare vivid impressions standing out against a field of forgetting, and persistence is presented as the only way to work through it.
rows.text_content: Memory is described as sparse and episodic, with rare vivid impressions standing out against a field of forgetting, and persistence is presented as the only way to work through it.

rows.paragraph_text: A widened awareness is said to reveal another striking property, reported through Ouspensky and Gurdjieff and confirmed by the author's own repeated observation.
rows.text_content: A widened awareness is said to reveal another striking property, reported through Ouspensky and Gurdjieff and confirmed by the author's own repeated observation.

rows.paragraph_text: Personal life memories are characterized as sparse, uneven, and composed of two different qualities: vague, hard-to-place recollections and more deeply impressed experiences.
rows.text_content: Personal life memories are characterized as sparse, uneven, and composed of two different qualities: vague, hard-to-place recollections and more deeply impressed experiences.

content.free_text: book_page nonfiction_philosophy philosophy_of_consciousness Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2, section Perlen in der Dunkelheit. Reflective prose on forgetting, memory, widened awareness, and the contrast between vague and deeply impressed recollections.
